response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine systems-level work (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and strong testing culture, but he falls well short of the core minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ experience — his C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, with most of his career in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. He also lacks demonstrated formal technical-leadership experience mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers and has no production cloud compute platform ownership, making him a poor fit for this specific role despite solid overall engineering credentials.
strengths: Real high-scale distributed-systems experience: low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tools authorship, and teaching/workshops
Broad full-stack and infrastructure breadth (Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, event-driven platforms), plus long-running open-source project ownership
concerns: C++ experience is far below the 8-year minimum requirement — primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No formal technical-lead or cloud/DevOps mentoring role; leadership evidence is informal (workshops, founder of a small OSS project)
No direct experience building or operating an internal cloud compute platform at scale

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. hard requirement
Lack of formal technical-leadership track record
